pattern: Fix the count of the first item, then the remaining budget divided by the second price (plus the zero option) counts the choices for the second, summed over all feasible first-item counts.
hints: If you decide how many pens to buy, the money left determines how many pencils are possible.
For a fixed number of pens, the number of pencil choices is the leftover budget divided by the pencil price, plus one for buying zero pencils.
Loop the pen count from zero while pens times cost1 stays within total, summing (leftover // cost2) + 1 each time.

edge_inputs.description: both items unaffordable, only the buy-nothing way
exactly one pen affordable, no pencils
pencils very cheap so many choices per pen count
pen price one keeps the outer loop large
minimum total still has one way

stress.description: random budgets and prices kept moderate so the outer loop stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = rng.randint(1, min(size, 100000))
    cost1 = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    cost2 = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [total, cost1, cost2]
stress.note: cost1 as low as 1 makes the loop run total times; both references use O(1) inner arithmetic so this stays well under the timeout
